executive_snapshot: Albuquerque scores 5.0 (WATCHLIST) for growth operators, driven by high charter saturation (54 schools) offsetting strong academic need (43% ELA, 25% math proficiency). Two accessible authorizers (PEC and APS) and proven multi-campus operators create replication-friendly conditions. Key risk: market saturation limits competitive opportunity. Key opportunity: quality gap in district performance creates demand for high-performing alternatives.

override_flags.triggered_by: Strong replication readiness AND favorable authorizer

top_drivers.driver: 54 active charter schools signal a mature, potentially saturated market where new entrants face intense competition for enrollment and differentiation.
District proficiency rates below 50% in ELA and 25% in math create demand for higher-performing alternatives, particularly in underserved neighborhoods.
Dual authorizer access (state PEC and local APS) provides pathway flexibility for operators with different growth strategies and community ties.

dimension_table.driver: Low district proficiency creates demand for quality alternatives, especially in math where only 1 in 4 students meets standards.
54 charter schools represent significant market penetration; new entrants must demonstrate clear differentiation or superior performance.
73,000-student district provides scale, but enrollment trend data unavailable to assess growth trajectory or demographic shifts.
Neutral-to-supportive environment based on recent school board composition; no evidence of hostile policy shifts or charter restrictions.
PEC state authorizer approves majority of schools; APS district authorizer offers local pathway for community-rooted operators.
No verified facilities data available; urban market assumption suggests moderate availability but requires local market research.
Established multi-campus operators (Mission Achievement, ACES Technical, South Valley Prep) demonstrate proven replication pathways and operational infrastructure.
No verified philanthropic or grant data available; operators should assess local foundation landscape and state funding stability.
Quality gap exists but high charter density limits white space; success requires differentiated model or superior execution in underserved segments.
No verified ELL/IEP data available; urban district likely presents moderate-to-high complexity requiring specialized student support capacity.

recommendations.action: Pursue PEC authorization pathway for operators with proven track records in similar urban markets seeking statewide scale.
recommendations.rationale: PEC approves majority of charters and offers state-level credibility; established operators like Mission Achievement and ACES Technical demonstrate successful multi-campus expansion through this route.
recommendations.evidence_summary: PEC state authorizer approves most schools; multiple operators run 2+ campuses successfully.
recommendations.primary_risk: High charter saturation (54 schools) means PEC may prioritize differentiated models or underserved geographic pockets over general replication.

recommendations.action: Target math-focused or STEM models to address 24.58% proficiency gap, positioning as district performance alternative.
recommendations.rationale: Math proficiency significantly lags ELA (24.58% vs 43.07%), creating acute need. Quality gap documented in district performance data provides competitive positioning opportunity.
recommendations.evidence_summary: District math proficiency at 24.58%, well below ELA; quality gap verified by state data.
recommendations.primary_risk: 54 existing charters may already serve high-demand segments; market research required to identify underserved neighborhoods or grade bands.

quick_reads.facilities: No verified facilities data available for Albuquerque. Urban market suggests commercial real estate options exist, but operators must independently assess availability, cost, and zoning. Facilities feasibility scored 5.0 (neutral) by default due to data gap.
quick_reads.political: Political climate index of 6 indicates neutral-to-supportive environment based on 2025 school board composition. No evidence of hostile charter legislation or district opposition. Moderate confidence due to reliance on single media source; local stakeholder interviews recommended.
quick_reads.authorizer: Two authorizers serve Albuquerque: PEC (state-level, approves majority) and APS (district-level, local focus). PEC pathway suits operators seeking statewide scale; APS pathway suits community-rooted applicants. Both accessible, with established approval track records.
